title: Microsorum parksii
taxonomy: Microsorum parksii is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of microsorus, which is a group of small, compact ferns that are commonly found in tropical regions. Microsorum parksii is native to New Guinea and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant is known for its small, delicate fronds and ability to grow in a variety of environments. As a member of the Polypodiaceae family, Microsorum parksii is closely related to other species of microsorus.
